title: At the Hair Salon
summary: A client visits a hair salon and discusses the cut and colour she wants with her hairdresser.

body.speaker: Client
body.text: Hi, I have an appointment at two o'clock. My name is Sophie Carter.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: G'day, Sophie! Come on in. I'm Natasha, and I'll be looking after you today. Take a seat.

body.speaker: Client
body.text: Thanks. I'm so glad to be here — my hair is a complete mess.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Don't worry, we'll sort it out! So, what are we doing today? Just a trim, or something more?

body.speaker: Client
body.text: I'd like a proper cut, actually. I want to go quite a bit shorter — maybe up to my shoulders.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: That would look great on you. Do you want to keep it all one length, or would you like some layers?

body.speaker: Client
body.text: Layers would be nice — something that gives it a bit more volume and movement.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Perfect. And what about colour? Are you happy with your natural colour, or would you like a change?

body.speaker: Client
body.text: I was thinking about adding some highlights. Nothing too dramatic — just some lighter tones around the face.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Ooh, I love that idea. Some soft golden highlights would really brighten your face. Shall I show you some pictures?

body.speaker: Client
body.text: Yes please! I want to make sure we're thinking of the same thing before you start.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Good thinking. Here — what do you think of this style? It's a shoulder-length cut with soft highlights.

body.speaker: Client
body.text: Oh, I love that! That's almost exactly what I had in mind.

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Wonderful. I'll start with the colour first, then do the cut. The whole thing should take about two hours.

body.speaker: Client
body.text: That's fine. Can I get a coffee while I wait?

body.speaker: Hairdresser
body.text: Of course! I'll grab you one right now. Make yourself comfortable.

annotations.meaningVi: một mớ hỗn độn hoàn toàn / rất lộn xộn
annotations.exampleEn: My desk is a complete mess — I need to tidy it up.
Don't worry, I'll sort it out by the end of the day.

annotations.meaningVi: một lần cắt tóc thực sự (không chỉ tỉa gọn)
annotations.exampleEn: I haven't had a proper cut in months.

annotations.meaningVi: cắt ngắn hẳn đi một chút
annotations.exampleEn: I'm going to go quite a bit shorter this time.
Layers give your hair more movement and body.

annotations.meaningVi: độ dày và sự chuyển động (của tóc)
annotations.exampleEn: This shampoo adds volume and movement to fine hair.

annotations.meaningVi: tóc nhuộm sáng (một số lọn tóc nhạt hơn)
annotations.exampleEn: She had golden highlights added to her brown hair.

annotations.meaningVi: không quá nổi bật / không quá táo bạo
annotations.exampleEn: I want a change, but nothing too dramatic — just a small difference.

annotations.meaningVi: làm sáng màu tóc xung quanh khuôn mặt
annotations.exampleEn: Some lighter tones around the face can brighten your look.

annotations.meaningVi: đã nghĩ đến / đang có ý định
annotations.exampleEn: Is this the style you had in mind?
Please make yourself comfortable while you wait.

grammarNotes.title: 'Would you like' vs 'Do you want': polite questions in service situations
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Would you like...?' là cách hỏi lịch sự và chuyên nghiệp, thường dùng trong dịch vụ như tiệm làm tóc, nhà hàng, cửa hàng. 'Would you like some layers?' / 'Would you like a change?' Lịch sự hơn so với 'Do you want layers?' Khi trả lời, bạn có thể nói 'Yes, please' / 'No, thank you' hoặc giải thích chi tiết hơn. Trong bài, khách hàng cũng dùng 'I'd like a proper cut' — cũng là cách lịch sự tương tự.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Would you like...?' is the polite, professional way to make offers and ask about preferences in service situations. Examples: 'Would you like some layers?' / 'Would you like a change?' It is more polite than 'Do you want...?' In the same way, 'I'd like...' (I would like) is the polite form for making requests: 'I'd like a proper cut.' These forms are standard in any customer service interaction.

grammarNotes.title: 'Shall I' for making suggestions and offers
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Shall I + verb nguyên thể' được dùng để đề nghị làm điều gì đó cho người khác hoặc gợi ý một hành động. 'Shall I show you some pictures?' — Tôi có nên cho bạn xem một số hình ảnh không? Đây thường dùng ở ngôi thứ nhất (I/we): 'Shall I open the window?' / 'Shall we start?' Khác với 'Should I' — 'Should I' thường hỏi về điều đúng nên làm, còn 'Shall I' thường là đề nghị lịch sự.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Shall I + bare infinitive' to make a polite offer to do something for someone. Example: 'Shall I show you some pictures?' It is typically used with first person (I/we): 'Shall I get you a coffee?' / 'Shall we start now?' 'Shall I' is a genuine offer — you are asking if the other person wants you to do something. It is more common in British English than American English, where 'Should I' or 'Do you want me to?' are more typical.

translationVi:
Khách hàng: Xin chào, tôi có lịch hẹn lúc hai giờ. Tên tôi là Sophie Carter.
Thợ làm tóc: Chào Sophie! Vào đây đi. Tôi là Natasha, và hôm nay tôi sẽ phục vụ bạn. Ngồi xuống đây nhé.
Khách hàng: Cảm ơn. Tôi rất vui được đến đây — tóc tôi rối bù cả rồi.
Thợ làm tóc: Đừng lo, chúng ta sẽ sắp xếp lại thôi! Vậy, hôm nay chúng ta làm gì? Chỉ tỉa gọn, hay gì khác hơn?
Khách hàng: Tôi muốn cắt tóc hẳn hoi, thực ra. Tôi muốn cắt ngắn hẳn — có thể đến vai.
Thợ làm tóc: Kiểu đó sẽ rất đẹp với bạn đấy. Bạn muốn để đều một kiểu hay muốn cắt nhiều lớp tầng?
Khách hàng: Cắt nhiều lớp sẽ hay hơn — kiểu gì tạo thêm độ dày và chuyển động cho tóc.
Thợ làm tóc: Hoàn hảo. Còn màu sắc thì sao? Bạn hài lòng với màu tóc tự nhiên, hay muốn thay đổi?
Khách hàng: Tôi đang nghĩ đến việc nhuộm highlight. Không quá nổi bật — chỉ một số tông màu sáng hơn xung quanh khuôn mặt.
Thợ làm tóc: Ồ, tôi thích ý tưởng đó. Một vài lọn highlight vàng nhẹ sẽ làm sáng khuôn mặt bạn lắm. Tôi có nên cho bạn xem một vài hình ảnh không?
Khách hàng: Vâng được ạ! Tôi muốn chắc chắn chúng ta đang nghĩ đến điều tương tự trước khi bắt đầu.
Thợ làm tóc: Đúng lắm. Đây — bạn nghĩ sao về kiểu tóc này? Đây là kiểu cắt đến vai với highlight nhẹ.
Khách hàng: Ồ, tôi thích quá! Đây gần như chính xác là điều tôi đang nghĩ đến.
Thợ làm tóc: Tuyệt vời. Tôi sẽ bắt đầu với nhuộm tóc trước, rồi mới cắt. Tất cả sẽ mất khoảng hai tiếng.
Khách hàng: Không sao. Tôi có thể lấy một tách cà phê trong lúc chờ không?
Thợ làm tóc: Tất nhiên! Để tôi lấy ngay cho bạn. Hãy ngồi thoải mái nhé.

questions.explanation: Sophie says 'I have an appointment at two o'clock.'
questions.hint: She mentions it when she arrives.

questions.prompt: How does Sophie describe her hair when she arrives?
questions.hint: She uses an expression meaning very untidy.

questions.prompt: How short does Sophie want her hair cut?
options: Very short like a pixie cut
questions.explanation: 'I want to go quite a bit shorter — maybe up to my shoulders.'
questions.hint: She mentions a specific body part.

questions.prompt: What style does Sophie choose for her cut?
questions.explanation: 'Layers would be nice — something that gives it a bit more volume and movement.'
questions.hint: She wants more volume and movement.

questions.prompt: What colour treatment does Sophie want?
options: A full colour change to blonde
questions.explanation: 'I was thinking about adding some highlights.'
questions.hint: She wants lighter tones, not darker ones.

questions.prompt: Where does Sophie want the lighter tones?
options: On the tips of her hair
questions.explanation: 'Just some lighter tones around the face.'
questions.hint: She wants it to brighten a specific area.

questions.prompt: Why does Sophie want to see pictures before Natasha starts?
options: To choose the exact colour number
To make sure they are thinking of the same thing
questions.explanation: 'I want to make sure we're thinking of the same thing before you start.'
questions.hint: She wants to confirm they share the same vision.

questions.prompt: What does Sophie think of the picture Natasha shows her?
options: She likes it but wants it shorter
She loves it — it is almost exactly what she had in mind
She thinks the highlights are too bright
questions.explanation: 'Oh, I love that! That's almost exactly what I had in mind.'
questions.hint: She is very positive about it.

questions.prompt: What will Natasha do first — the colour or the cut?
options: She will wash Sophie's hair first
questions.explanation: 'I'll start with the colour first, then do the cut.'

questions.prompt: How long will the appointment take in total?
questions.explanation: 'The whole thing should take about two hours.'

questions.prompt: What does Sophie ask for while she waits?
questions.explanation: 'Can I get a coffee while I wait?'

cloze.template:
Client: Hi, I have an {{b1}} at two o'clock. My name is Sophie Carter.
Hairdresser: G'day, Sophie! Come on in. I'm Natasha, and I'll be looking after you today. Take a seat.
Client: Thanks. My hair is a complete {{b2}}.
Hairdresser: Don't worry, we'll sort it out! So, what are we doing today? Just a {{b3}}, or something more?
Client: I'd like a proper cut. I want to go quite a bit {{b4}} — maybe up to my shoulders.
Hairdresser: That would look great on you. Do you want to keep it all one length, or would you like some {{b5}}?
Client: Layers would be nice — something that gives it a bit more volume and movement.
Hairdresser: And what about colour? Would you like a {{b6}}?
Client: I was thinking about adding some {{b7}}. Nothing too dramatic — just some lighter tones around the face.
Hairdresser: Some soft golden highlights would really {{b8}} your face. Shall I show you some pictures?
Client: Yes please! I want to make sure we're thinking of the same thing before you start.
Hairdresser: Here — what do you think of this style? It's a shoulder-length cut with soft highlights.
Client: Oh, I love that! That's almost exactly what I had in {{b9}}.
Hairdresser: Wonderful. I'll start with the colour first, then do the cut. The whole thing should take about two {{b10}}.
Client: That's fine. Can I get a coffee while I wait?
Hairdresser: Of course! Make yourself comfortable.

blanks.explanation: 'appointment' — you make an appointment at a hair salon.
'mess' — 'a complete mess' means very untidy or disorganised.
'trim' — a trim is a small cut to neaten the ends of the hair.
'shorter' — Sophie wants to 'go quite a bit shorter.'
'layers' — layers add volume and movement to the hair.
'change' — the hairdresser asks 'would you like a change?' regarding the colour.
'highlights' — Sophie wants to add lighter highlights to her hair.
'brighten' — the passage says highlights would 'brighten your face.'
'mind' — 'had in mind' means the idea or image you were thinking of.
'hours' — the appointment should take 'about two hours.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Sophie wants to change her hairstyle and add highlights. How important do you think appearance and style are to a person's confidence and identity? Do you think people should feel free to change their appearance as they wish, or are there situations where appearance should follow certain rules?
